title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend
text: LOWELL, Ark.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:JBHT) announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly dividend on its common stock of $ 0.42 (forty two cents) per common share. The dividend is payable to stockholders of record on August 4, 2023 and will be paid on August 18, 2023. About J.B. Hunt J.B. title: JB Hunt (JBHT) Reports Q2 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: The headline numbers for JB Hunt (JBHT) give insight into how the company performed in the quarter ended June 2023, but it may be worthwhile to compare some of its key metrics to Wall Street estimates and the year-ago actuals.

title: JB Hunt (JBHT) Misses Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: JB Hunt (JBHT) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.81 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.97 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.42 per share a year ago.

title: J.B. Hunt stock falls as continued weaker demand weighs on results
text: Shares of J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. JBHT, +2.61% fell after hours on Tuesday after the trucking, transportation and logistics provider reported second-quarter results that missed Wall Street's expectations, as weaker demand pulled sales lower.

title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. Reports U.S. GAAP Revenues, Net Earnings and Earnings Per Share for the Second Quarter 2023
text: LOWELL, Ark.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. (NASDAQ: JBHT) announced second quarter 2023 U.S. GAAP (United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) net earnings of $189.6 million, or diluted earnings per share of $1.81 versus second quarter 2022 net earnings of $255.3 million, or $2.42 per diluted share. title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services: Fantastic Business That Is Returning Quickly
text: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc saw a 7% YoY decrease in revenues and a drop in EPS from $2.29 to $1.89, but its share price continues to rise. The company's largest revenue streams are the Intermodal and Dedicated Contract Services segments, which represent 48% and 27% of total revenues, respectively. Despite a decrease in both top and bottom lines in Q1 2023, the company is expected to see EPS growth in Q2 2023 due to rising rates and stable gas prices.
